subjects.name: Social Science (Geography & Economics)
subjects.world_tagline: Master the terrain and wealth of the world.
subjects.world_description: A vast map of mountains, rivers, and markets. Managing natural resources and understanding the flow of money is key to survival here.

chapters.title: Natural Resources
chapters.boss_description: A ruthless entity digging up forests and mountains without any thought of tomorrow.
chapters.cutscene: The green lands are being stripped bare! Extractor the Greedy is destroying non-renewable deposits. Regulate the land and water use quickly!
chapters.completion_message: You secured the natural reserves for future generations!

concepts.title: Renewable Resources
concepts.explanation: Resources that can be naturally replenished quickly over time.
concepts.example: Solar energy and wind energy.
concepts.memory_tip: Renew = Make new again.

concepts.title: Non-Renewable
concepts.explanation: Resources that take millions of years to form. Once used, they are gone.
concepts.example: Coal, petroleum, and natural gas.
concepts.memory_tip: Gone forever.

concepts.title: Sustainable Development
concepts.explanation: Using resources carefully so we meet our current needs without ruining the future.
concepts.example: Planting a new tree for every one you cut down.
concepts.memory_tip: Sustain the future.

concepts.title: Soil Erosion
concepts.explanation: The removal of the top fertile layer of soil by wind or water.
concepts.example: Heavy rain washing away a farmer's field.
concepts.memory_tip: Soil running away.

mini_challenges.question: Which of these is a renewable resource?
mini_challenges.explanation: Wind is infinite.

mini_challenges.question: The Black soil is most suitable for growing...
mini_challenges.explanation: Black soil is known as black cotton soil.

mini_challenges.question: Terrace farming helps prevent...
mini_challenges.explanation: Flat steps on hills prevent soil from washing down.

boss_questions.question: Laterite soil develops in areas with...
boss_questions.explanation: Intense leaching causes laterite formation.
boss_questions.hint: Hot and wet.

boss_questions.question: Agenda 21 was adopted at which Summit?
boss_questions.explanation: Rio de Janeiro, 1992.
boss_questions.hint: Rio.

boss_questions.question: Most of India's land is used for...
boss_questions.explanation: India is an agrarian economy.
boss_questions.hint: Farming.

boss_questions.question: The state with the highest area under forest cover is...
boss_questions.explanation: MP has the largest forest cover geographically.
boss_questions.hint: Central India.

boss_questions.question: Alluvial soil is deposited by...
boss_questions.explanation: Rivers bring fine silt from mountains.
boss_questions.hint: Water.

important_questions.question: What is meant by Sustainable Development?
important_questions.answer: Development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.

important_questions.question: Explain Resource Planning.
important_questions.answer: Identifying resources, evolving a planning structure with technology, and matching it with national development plans.

important_questions.question: What is soil conservation?
important_questions.answer: Prevention of soil erosion using methods like terrace farming, contour ploughing, and planting shelter belts.

important_questions.question: Why is conservation of resources necessary?
important_questions.answer: Resources are limited, take millions of years to form, and over-utilization leads to environmental problems.

important_questions.question: Characteristics of Khadar soil.
important_questions.answer: It is the new alluvial soil. It is more fine and very fertile.

quick_revision.one_liner: Resources are the wealth of a nation, but they run out if not managed.
key_points: Alluvial soil is most common in India.
Laterite soil is poor in fertility due to leaching.
quick_revision.most_asked_topic: Soil erosion and types of soil.

previous_year_questions.question: What is black soil also known as?

chapters.title: Agriculture
chapters.boss_description: A scorching wind that dries up the monsoons and cracks the earth.
chapters.cutscene: The rains have failed! Protect the Kharif crops and set up irrigation channels to defeat Drought.
chapters.completion_message: The fields are green again!

concepts.title: Kharif Crops
concepts.explanation: Crops sown in the rainy season (June to Sept).
concepts.example: Rice, maize, and cotton.
concepts.memory_tip: Kharif = Kharab Weather (Monsoon).

concepts.title: Rabi Crops
concepts.explanation: Crops sown in winter (Oct to March).
concepts.example: Wheat, barley, and peas.
concepts.memory_tip: Rabi = Winter.

concepts.title: Subsistence Farming
concepts.explanation: Farming just enough to feed the farmer's own family, with nothing left to sell.
concepts.example: A small farmer growing wheat on half an acre just to eat.
concepts.memory_tip: Subsistence = Just surviving.

concepts.title: Commercial Farming
concepts.explanation: Large scale farming on huge estates meant entirely for selling in the market for profit.
concepts.example: Tea plantations in Assam.
concepts.memory_tip: Commercial = For commerce (money).

mini_challenges.question: Which of these is a Kharif crop?
mini_challenges.explanation: Rice needs lots of monsoon rain.

mini_challenges.question: Slash and burn agriculture is also known locally as...
mini_challenges.explanation: Jhumming in northeastern India.

mini_challenges.question: India is the largest producer of which crop globally?
mini_challenges.explanation: India leads in pulse production.

boss_questions.question: Which state is the largest producer of Jute?
boss_questions.explanation: Needs flooded fields and hot climate.
boss_questions.hint: East India.

boss_questions.question: What is the 'Golden Fiber'?
boss_questions.explanation: Jute has a golden yellowish color.
boss_questions.hint: Used for gunny bags.

boss_questions.question: White Revolution is related to...
boss_questions.explanation: Operation Flood increased milk supply.
boss_questions.hint: Cow.

boss_questions.question: Green Revolution primarily benefited the production of...
boss_questions.explanation: HYV seeds of wheat were very successful.
boss_questions.hint: Punjab's main crop.

boss_questions.question: Bhoodan-Gramdan movement was initiated by...
boss_questions.explanation: He convinced landlords to donate land to landless farmers.
boss_questions.hint: Vinoba.

important_questions.question: What is Plantation Agriculture?
important_questions.answer: A type of commercial farming where a single crop (like tea, coffee, rubber) is grown on a large estate.

important_questions.question: Explain Zaid season.
important_questions.answer: A short season during summer months between Rabi and Kharif. Example crops: watermelon, muskmelon, cucumber.
important_questions.examiner_tip: Summer between the two main seasons.

important_questions.question: What are institutional reforms in agriculture?
important_questions.answer: Abolition of Zamindari system, consolidation of holdings, collectivization, and establishing grameen banks for low-interest loans.

important_questions.question: Why is agriculture the backbone of Indian economy?
important_questions.answer: It provides employment to more than half the population and supplies raw materials to industries.

important_questions.question: Conditions required for growing wheat.
important_questions.answer: Requires cool growing season and bright sunshine at ripening. Needs 50 to 75 cm of annual rainfall.

quick_revision.one_liner: India's seasons dictate its crops.
quick_revision.most_asked_topic: Differences between commercial and subsistence farming.

chapters.title: Manufacturing Industries
chapters.boss_description: A mechanised tycoon who values production over the respiratory health of the citizens.
chapters.cutscene: Factories are churning out goods, but the rivers run black! Balance industrial growth with environmental protection.
chapters.completion_message: You optimized the manufacturing plants and installed pollution filters!

concepts.title: Agro-based Industries
concepts.explanation: Industries that get their raw materials directly from agriculture.
concepts.example: Cotton textile mills, sugar factories.
concepts.memory_tip: Agro = Farm.

concepts.title: Mineral-based Industries
concepts.explanation: Industries that use processed minerals as raw materials.
concepts.example: Iron and steel plants.
concepts.memory_tip: Mineral = Rocks.

concepts.title: Basic Industries
concepts.explanation: Industries whose products or raw materials are used to manufacture OTHER goods.
concepts.example: Iron and steel industry makes steel, which is then used to make cars.
concepts.memory_tip: Basic = Foundation for others.

concepts.title: Consumer Industries
concepts.explanation: Industries making goods for direct use by consumers.
concepts.example: Toothpaste, paper, sewing machines.
concepts.memory_tip: Consumer = Direct buyer.

mini_challenges.question: Iron and Steel is an example of which type of industry?
mini_challenges.explanation: It provides steel to all other industries.

mini_challenges.question: Which city is known as the electronic capital of India?
mini_challenges.explanation: Bengaluru is the IT hub.

mini_challenges.question: Which of these is a joint sector industry?
mini_challenges.explanation: OIL is jointly owned by public and private sectors.

boss_questions.question: NTPC stands for...
boss_questions.explanation: Generates electricity.
boss_questions.hint: Thermal Power.

boss_questions.question: First successful cotton textile mill was established in?
boss_questions.explanation: Set up in 1854.
boss_questions.hint: Financial capital.

boss_questions.question: TISCO is located at...
boss_questions.explanation: Tata Iron and Steel Company.
boss_questions.hint: Tata.

boss_questions.question: BPO stands for?
boss_questions.explanation: IT industry term.
boss_questions.hint: Outsourcing.

boss_questions.question: Which gas leaked during the Bhopal tragedy in 1984?
boss_questions.explanation: MIC gas leak killed thousands.
boss_questions.hint: Methyl.

important_questions.question: What is the importance of manufacturing?
important_questions.answer: It modernizes agriculture, provides jobs, reduces dependence on farming, and brings foreign exchange by exporting goods.

important_questions.question: Define heavy and light industries.
important_questions.answer: Heavy industries use heavy raw materials and produce heavy goods (Iron & Steel). Light use light materials and produce light goods (Electrical fans).

important_questions.question: What are public sector industries?
important_questions.answer: Industries owned and operated by government agencies (e.g., BHEL, SAIL).

important_questions.question: How do industries pollute water?
important_questions.answer: By dumping untreated organic and inorganic industrial wastes into rivers.

important_questions.question: What is an Information Technology industry?
important_questions.answer: Industries dealing with software, telecommunication, and BPOs.

quick_revision.one_liner: Factories turn raw materials into wealth but create pollution if mismanaged.
key_points: Iron and steel is the basic industry of all.

common_mistakes: Confusing Basic industry and Heavy industry.

chapters.title: Tourism, Transport and Communication
chapters.boss_description: A chaotic tangle of blocked trade routes and dead internet connections.
chapters.cutscene: The kingdom's supply lines are choked! Build superhighways and establish satellite links.
chapters.completion_message: Trade paths cleared!

concepts.title: Roadways
concepts.explanation: Provide door-to-door service and connect remote areas better than trains.
concepts.example: Golden Quadrilateral connecting Delhi-Kolkata-Chennai-Mumbai.
concepts.memory_tip: Roads go everywhere.

concepts.title: Railways
concepts.explanation: Best for carrying heavy freight and massive populations over long distances cheaply.
concepts.example: Indian Railways.
concepts.memory_tip: Iron tracks for heavy loads.

concepts.title: Pipelines
concepts.explanation: Underground pipes directly transporting oil, gas, and water without delays.
concepts.example: Pipes from refineries to cities.
concepts.memory_tip: Hidden highways.

concepts.title: Tourism
concepts.explanation: An invisible trade that brings foreign exchange and supports local handicrafts.
concepts.example: Tourists paying to see the Taj Mahal.
concepts.memory_tip: Travel brings money.

mini_challenges.question: Which of the following is the cheapest mode of transport for bulky cargo?
mini_challenges.explanation: Boats are extremely fuel-efficient for heavy cargo.

mini_challenges.question: What connects the 4 mega cities of India?
mini_challenges.explanation: A massive highway project.

mini_challenges.question: Air India is what type of transport?
mini_challenges.explanation: Airlines.

boss_questions.question: Which port is the oldest artificial port of India?
boss_questions.explanation: Built by the British.
boss_questions.hint: Southern city.

boss_questions.question: What does STD stand for in telecommunication?
boss_questions.explanation: Calling across trunk routes.
boss_questions.hint: Subscriber.

boss_questions.question: Difference between Broad gauge and Meter gauge?
boss_questions.explanation: Broad is 1.676m.
boss_questions.hint: Width.

boss_questions.question: The longest National Highway in India?
boss_questions.explanation: Runs from Srinagar to Kanyakumari.
boss_questions.hint: Double 4.

boss_questions.question: Tourism is considered an 'invisible trade' because...
options: Services are exported, not physical goods
boss_questions.explanation: You export experiences, not boxes.
boss_questions.hint: Nothing packed in a box.

important_questions.question: Why are means of transport called lifelines of a nation?
important_questions.answer: They transport raw materials to factories, finished goods to markets, and connect people.

important_questions.question: Advantages of Roadways over Railways?
important_questions.answer: Less construction cost, can climb mountains, provides door-to-door service.

important_questions.question: What is mass communication?
important_questions.answer: Reaching a large audience using TV, radio, newspapers.

important_questions.question: Describe International Trade.
important_questions.answer: Exchange of goods and services between two or more countries.

important_questions.question: Why is Pipeline transport useful?
important_questions.answer: No delays, low running costs, no trans-shipment losses for oil/gas.

quick_revision.one_liner: Connecting people and goods makes an economy grow.

chapters.title: Population (Eco)
chapters.boss_description: A swarming entity stretching resources too thin.
chapters.cutscene: Too many people, too little food! Analyze the census.
chapters.completion_message: Stabilized the birth rates!

concepts.title: Population Density
concepts.explanation: Number of people living per square kilometer.
concepts.example: Delhi has high density.
concepts.memory_tip: Packed together.

mini_challenges.question: What is the census?
mini_challenges.explanation: Counted every 10 years.

boss_questions.question: Literacy rate measures...
boss_questions.explanation: Ability to read and write.
boss_questions.hint: School.

important_questions.question: Why is population an asset?
important_questions.answer: Healthy and educated people form valuable human resources.

quick_revision.one_liner: A population is an asset if educated, a liability if not.

chapters.title: Disaster Management
chapters.boss_description: Chaos incarnate manifesting as quakes and floods.
chapters.cutscene: Evacuate the city! Plan effectively.
chapters.completion_message: Casualties minimized!

concepts.title: First Aid
concepts.explanation: Immediate help before doctors arrive.
concepts.example: Applying pressure to a wound.
concepts.memory_tip: First line of defense.

mini_challenges.question: What is CPR?
mini_challenges.explanation: Life saving technique.

boss_questions.question: NDRF stands for?
boss_questions.explanation: Rescue force.
boss_questions.hint: Force.

important_questions.question: What is disaster management?
important_questions.answer: Preparation and response to natural or man-made disasters.

quick_revision.one_liner: Be prepared.

chapters.title: Economic Planning
chapters.boss_description: Vampiric force absorbing the GDP.
chapters.cutscene: The reserves are dry! Plan the 5-year budget.
chapters.completion_message: Economy stabilized!

concepts.title: Five Year Plans
concepts.explanation: Economic goals set for 5 years.
concepts.example: Focusing on agriculture.
concepts.memory_tip: Plan for 5.

mini_challenges.question: Who dissolved the Planning Commission?
mini_challenges.explanation: NITI Aayog replaced it.

boss_questions.question: What is NITI?
boss_questions.explanation: Think tank.
boss_questions.hint: Transforming.

important_questions.question: Role of NITI Aayog?
important_questions.answer: Policy think-tank for India.

quick_revision.one_liner: Planning creates wealth.

chapters.title: Indian Economy
chapters.boss_description: A mountain of debt crushing growth.
chapters.cutscene: Balance the trade ledgers!
chapters.completion_message: GDP is positive!

concepts.title: GDP
concepts.explanation: Gross Domestic Product. Total value of goods.
concepts.example: All sales in India.
concepts.memory_tip: Total money.

mini_challenges.question: Primary sector includes?
mini_challenges.explanation: Nature.

boss_questions.question: Tertiary sector is also called?
boss_questions.explanation: Provides services.
boss_questions.hint: Service.

important_questions.question: What is LPG?
important_questions.answer: Liberalization, Privatization, Globalization reforms of 1991.

quick_revision.one_liner: Economy spans nature, factories, and services.
